author: hardmaru
text:
One of my favorite findings: Positional embeddings are just training wheels. They help convergence but hurt long-context generalization.

We found that if you simply delete them after pretraining and recalibrate for < 1% of the original budget, you unlock massive context windows.

author: hardmaru
text: On the topic ‘influencers’ taking advantage of the “AGI Hype” cycle:

author: hardmaru
text:
The crazy part is that I’ve noticed many of these AI hype “influencers” claim on their Twitter or LinkedIn bios that they worked at Google Brain or DeepMind, when a simple fact check reveals that they are clearly lying. Wonder how many they fooled.

Be careful about your sources.

author: hardmaru
text: I’m so tired of AI hype tweets. They’re all over my Twitter feed these days.

author: hardmaru
text: Fantastic work. We need more exploration into these directions, pushing the Pareto efficient frontier of performance, rather than simply scaling everything up into gigantic models with resource and energy requirements approaching the magnitude of a large power plant.

author: hardmaru
text: My prediction is that longer term, we will discover ways to reduce something like a dense, 175B parameter large language model into one with only 3-4B parameters, without any noticeable reduction in capabilities.
